title: UK appoints Amy Barklam as new Trade Commissioner for LATAC
body: " Amy Barklam has been appointed His Majesty’s Trade Commissioner to Latin America & the Caribbean and Consul General São Paulo UK exports to the region are up over 50% in current prices, and Latin America & the Caribbean’s investment stock in the UK has accelerated to over £126bn at the end of 2023 Barklam will join team of nine HM Trade Commissioners promoting Global Britain across the world and encouraging UK trade and investment   The Department for Business and Trade (DBT) has announced the appointment of Amy Barklam as His Majesty’s Trade Commissioner (HMTC) for Latin America & the Caribbean (LATAC) and Consul General São Paulo, succeeding Jonathan Knott.  As the new Trade Commissioner for LATAC, Amy will generate business opportunities for the UK while contributing to the growth of sustainable, resilient, and productive economies across LATAC.   HMTCs play a vital role in delivering the UK Government’s growth mission by championing British business on the global stage. Positioned in key international markets, HMTCs facilitate international partnerships in high growth industries.  By promoting the UK’s strengths they help secure new opportunities for British industries, support job creation at home, and reinforce the UK’s reputation as a world-leading destination for trade and investment.  Amy will bring extensive experience of international business, executive relationship management, investment banking and governance to the role. Amy’s career outside Government has included senior leadership positions at global institutions including HSBC, Morgan Stanley and DTZ (now Cushman & Wakefield).  Outside of her corporate work Amy is deeply engaged in non-profit governance and advocacy. She is the Board Chair of Days for Girls UK, a Non-Executive Director at Project Accountability, and Awards Chair for the University of Illinois Alumni Association.  Jonathan will leave post on 23 January 2026 and Amy will complete language training before taking up the role on 27 April 2026. Oliver Ballhatchet will be Acting HMTC in the interim period.  Amy Barklam, Designate Trade Commissioner for Latin America & the Caribbean, and Consul General São Paulo, said:  “I am truly honoured to have been appointed His Majesty’s Trade Commissioner for Latin America and the Caribbean, and Consul General in São Paulo. It is a privilege to return to the region where I began my career and to work with partners across government and business to deepen our historic ties and further expand opportunities for trade and investment.  “The region offers enormous potential for sustainable growth, innovation and collaboration. Together with the dedicated LATAC team, I look forward to deepening existing relationships, building new ones, and supporting the delivery of HMG’s Industrial and Trade Strategies across this dynamic region.”  Jonathan Knott, His Majesty’s Trade Commissioner for Latin America & the Caribbean, and Consul General São Paulo, said:  “It has been a great privilege and pleasure to serve as HMTC for Latin America and the Caribbean over the last 5 years. The talented, energetic and expert team which I have led has achieved some great successes, enabling the UK’s commercial relationship with the countries of the region to grow stronger than ever.   “UK...
